summary: Bead 1's concrete implementation is sound and reuses the right primitives. (1) PRIMITIVES: resolveDomains (ownership.go:211), listDomainDirs (docsync.go:351), loadOwnershipForRef (ownership.go:221), LoadOwnership (78), matchesAny (269), GlobMatch (294) all exist in internal/validate with the cited signatures; a new file in package validate calls them with ZERO new exports and no new cross-package edge. Placement in internal/validate (not internal/ownership) is correct — all reuse targets and both consumer files (divergence.go, plan.go) already live there; internal/ownership doesn't own these. The proposed helper sig normalizeImpactedDomains(exec, root, ownerRef, entries) mirrors attributeDomain's plumbing exactly. (2) WIRING: divergence.go:142 really is candidateDomains := append([]string(nil), meta.Domains...), with exec/root/ownerRef all in scope at the ValidateDivergence boundary — clean swap, empty-fallback (143-152) and the attributeDomain loop (187) preserved. In plan.go the single loadImpactedDomains call at L124 feeds BOTH checkADRCitations (L146) AND checkADRCoverage (L156), so normalizing once at the ValidatePlan call site propagates to both plan-gates automatically — traced and confirmed. ValidatePlan(root,specID) has no executor, but the plan correctly anticipates this: ownerRef=='' routes resolveDomains/loadOwnershipForRef to the on-disk LoadOwnership/listDomainDirs branches which ignore exec (may be nil), so the plan path needs no executor. R2 message lives in checkADRCoverage notCovered case at L529 with citations in scope for len()-branching — correct. (3) GUARD: normalization resolves the DECLARED set in place, never widening to all-domains. TestCompleteRejectsUndeclaredDomainTouch declares ['payments','search'] (both real domain dirs → kept verbatim), changed file internal/payments/charge.go still attributes to payments (in set) → adr-divergence-uncovered → stays green. No attribution widening. (4) ERRORS: 0-owner/>1-owner return as error slice, surfaced via r.AddError on both paths (divergence: early-return; plan: impacted-domains-resolve diagnostic) — clean fail, no panic/silent-drop. >1-owner is correctly a future-overlap guard: real manifests are disjoint (each claims distinct internal/<pkg>/** trees), so the multi-owner test uses the synthetic alpha+beta overlap fixture (ownership_test.go:26-27). Note the helper's all-owners-enumeration is a deliberate NEW stricter policy vs attributeDomain's first-match-wins — intentional, not a misuse. (5) ADR-0032 edit: current Domain(s)=validation,adr,lifecycle (no workflow); adding workflow is one coherent doc edit in Bead 1. No self-trap: the ADR doc is .mindspec/docs/** → isDocFile→isProcessArtifact→skipped by divergence (no OWNERSHIP claim needed), and the spec deliberately omits ADR-0032 from frontmatter citations so coverage/citation gates never evaluate it. The amendment note + Domain(s) edit are the same single change. (6) OTHER CALLERS: loadImpactedDomains has exactly ONE caller (plan.go:124) — routing there is fully contained. budgeter.go:180 and domain/show.go:85 read meta.Domains by calling contextpack.ParseSpec directly, NOT through the validate gate helper; since the plan normalizes only at the gate layer and leaves ParseSpec/contextpack dumb (returns raw entries), these keep raw entries. No leak, no foot-gun. workflow owns internal/validate/** so the new helper file is correctly scoped to this spec's declared domain.
